compiled_state.title: test-batched.sh runner: harvest-worktree + merge-to-main tests fail under batch but pass standalone (shared state pollution)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description: test_complete_comment_written_on_success and test_complete_comment_written_on_failure in test-harvest-worktree.sh: FAILED 4 under batched runner, PASSED 39 standalone. test-merge-to-main-auto-resolve.sh, test-merge-to-main-cleanliness.sh, test-merge-to-main-portability.sh also fail batched/pass standalone. Root cause: likely shared git/ticket/branch state pollution between tests in batched execution. Blocks commits for f6c6-362c, d888-632b, a44a-0f63.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 4 (INTERMEDIATE) — severity=1 (blocks commits), complexity=2 (cross-test state), environment=0 (local-only), intermittent=1 (env-specific: batch vs standalone). Single coherent issue.
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Investigation complete. Root cause: tests 18/19 (test_complete_comment_written_on_success, test_complete_comment_written_on_failure) were RED tests for unimplemented WORKTREE_TRACKING:complete functionality. harvest-worktree.sh lacked --ticket-id parameter and _DSO_SHIM resolution. Commit 19523fb4 ('feat(worktree-tracking): implement WORKTREE_TRACKING lifecycle signals') implemented both features with proper PATH fallback for test stub injection. All 337 test files + 11 merge-to-main tests now pass in batch mode. The test-batched.sh runner shows PASSED 39 FAILED 0 for harvest-worktree tests in isolation and PASSED 66 FAILED 0 for all 11 merge-to-main test files.
comments.author: Test
